sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The Google Drive connector integrates with the **Google Drive API v3** to sync files and shared drives into the Shielva knowledge base. It is designed for teams that store documentation, design assets, spreadsheets, and other content in Google Drive and need that content searchable and indexed in Shielva.

**Key capabilities:**
- Full file listing with automatic pagination via `nextPageToken`
- Folder and shared drive enumeration
- Single-file metadata retrieval by file ID
- Drive query language support for filtered file searches (e.g. `name contains 'report'`, `mimeType = 'application/pdf'`)
- Export of Google Workspace documents (Docs, Sheets, Slides) to binary formats such as `text/plain` or `application/pdf`
- File permission inspection per file ID
- OAuth2 with `access_type=offline` — Google issues a refresh token so the connector never requires re-authorization just because an access token expired
- SHA-256 stable document IDs for idempotent ingestion into the knowledge base
- Automatic retry with up to 3 attempts per API call on transient failures
- Multi-tenant: every normalized document is stamped with `connector_id` and `tenant_id`

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### Prerequisites
- A Google Cloud project with the **Google Drive API** enabled (Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Library)
- OAuth 2.0 credentials of type **Web Application** created under APIs & Services → Credentials
- The Shielva redirect URI added to the OAuth client's **Authorized redirect URIs**

### Step 1 — Install the connector

Provide your OAuth2 credentials:

```python
import asyncio
from connector import GoogleDriveConnector

connector = GoogleDriveConnector(
    tenant_id="tenant-abc",
    connector_id="gdrive-01",
    config={
        "client_id": "xxxxxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxx.apps.googleusercontent.com",
        "client_secret": "GOCSPX-...",
        "redirect_uri": "https://app.shielva.ai/connectors/callback",
    },
)
result = asyncio.run(connector.install())
print(result.health, result.message)
# ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY  Connector installed — complete OAuth to connect
```

### Step 2 — Authorize with Google

```python
auth_url = asyncio.run(connector.authorize())
# Redirect the user's browser to auth_url.
# After consent, Google calls back with ?code=...
```

### Step 3 — Exchange the authorization code

```python
token_data = asyncio.run(connector.exchange_code(auth_code="4/0AfGeNi..."))
# token_data contains access_token and refresh_token — store both.
```

### Step 4 — Verify connectivity

```python
health = asyncio.run(connector.health_check())
print(health.health, health.user_email)
# ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY  user@example.com
```

### Step 5 — Run your first sync

```python
result = asyncio.run(connector.sync(kb_id="my-knowledge-base"))
print(f"{result.status}: {result.documents_synced}/{result.documents_found} files synced")
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
The connector uses **OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow** with `access_type=offline` so Google issues a long-lived refresh token alongside the short-lived (1-hour) access token.

### Flow

1. **Install** — provide `client_id` and `client_secret` (validated for presence only; no network call at this stage).
2. **Authorize** — call `authorize()` to generate the Google consent URL. Parameters embedded:
   - `response_type=code`
   - `scope=https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.metadata.readonly`
   - `access_type=offline` — triggers refresh token issuance
   - `prompt=consent` — forces Google to re-issue the refresh token even for previously authorized apps
3. **Exchange** — after user consent, Google redirects to `redirect_uri?code=...`. Call `exchange_code(auth_code)` to POST to `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token` and receive `access_token` + `refresh_token`.
4. **Auto-refresh** — when the access token expires, the internal `_do_refresh_token()` method posts the stored `refresh_token` to the token endpoint to obtain a new `access_token` silently.

### Endpoints

| Step | Method | URL |
|------|--------|-----|
| Authorization redirect | GET (browser) | `https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth` |
| Token exchange / refresh | POST | `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token` |

### OAuth2 scopes

| Scope | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly` | Read all files, file content, and shared drives |
| `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.metadata.readonly` | Read file metadata only (used for listing and health check) |

### Required config keys

| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `client_id` | Found in Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials → your OAuth 2.0 Client ID |
| `client_secret` | Found alongside `client_id`; format starts with `GOCSPX-` |
| `redirect_uri` | Must exactly match one Authorized Redirect URI registered in the Google Cloud Console OAuth client |

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
Pass all fields in the `config` dict when constructing `GoogleDriveConnector`.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `client_id` | string | Yes | OAuth2 Client ID from Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Format: `xxxxxxxxxxxx-xxxxxxxx.apps.googleusercontent.com` |
| `client_secret` | password | Yes | OAuth2 Client Secret found alongside `client_id`. Format starts with `GOCSPX-`. Stored encrypted by Shielva. |
| `redirect_uri` | string | No | The OAuth2 redirect URI registered in Google Cloud Console. Defaults to the Shielva platform callback (`https://app.shielva.ai/connectors/callback`) if omitted. |
| `access_token` | string | No | Populated automatically after `exchange_code()`. Pass to resume an already-authorized session. |
| `refresh_token` | string | No | Populated automatically after `exchange_code()`. Used internally to refresh expired access tokens. |
| `base_url` | string | No | Override the Drive API base URL. Defaults to `https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3`. Useful for pointing at a mock server in tests. |

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All methods are `async` and must be awaited. `GoogleDriveConnector` inherits from `BaseConnector` and adds Drive-specific file and folder operations.

children.title: install()
children.content:
Validates that `client_id` and `client_secret` are present in config. Makes **no network request** — credential presence is sufficient at install time. Full connectivity is confirmed after OAuth authorization.

**Returns:** `InstallResult`
- `health`: `ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY` if credentials present, `OFFLINE` if missing
- `auth_status`: `AuthStatus.CONNECTED` or `AuthStatus.MISSING_CREDENTIALS`
- `connector_id`: echoed from constructor
- `message`: `'Connector installed — complete OAuth to connect'` on success

```python
connector = GoogleDriveConnector(
    tenant_id="tenant-abc",
    connector_id="gdrive-01",
    config={"client_id": "...", "client_secret": "..."},
)
result = await connector.install()
# InstallResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED,
#   message='Connector installed — complete OAuth to connect')
```

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
Calls `GET /about?fields=user,storageQuota` against the Drive API to verify the access token is valid and the API is reachable. Retries up to 2 times on transient errors.

**Returns:** `HealthCheckResult`
- `health`: `HEALTHY`, `DEGRADED`, or `OFFLINE`
- `auth_status`: `CONNECTED`, `TOKEN_EXPIRED`, or `FAILED`
- `user_email`: Google account email of the authenticated user
- `storage_quota`: dict with `limit`, `usage`, and `usageInDrive` keys (bytes as strings)

```python
health = await connector.health_check()
if health.health.value == "healthy":
    print(f"Connected as {health.user_email}")
    print(f"Drive usage: {health.storage_quota.get('usageInDrive')} bytes")
```

children.title: sync()
children.content:
Pages through all files in Drive using `nextPageToken` until exhausted (page size 100, up to 3 retries per page). Each file is normalized via `normalize_file()` into a `ConnectorDocument` with a SHA-256 stable ID and ingested into the knowledge base.

**Parameters:**
- `kb_id` (str, optional) — Knowledge base ID to ingest documents into
- `full` (bool, optional) — Reserved; currently performs a full scan regardless
- `webhook_url` (str, optional) — Reserved for future webhook-based ingest

**Returns:** `SyncResult`
- `status`: `SyncStatus.COMPLETED` (zero failures) or `PARTIAL` (some failures) or `FAILED`
- `documents_found`: total files discovered across all pages
- `documents_synced`: successfully normalized and ingested
- `documents_failed`: files that raised an exception during normalization
- `message`: summary string

```python
result = await connector.sync(kb_id="kb-production")
print(f"{result.status}: {result.documents_synced}/{result.documents_found} synced")
# SyncStatus.COMPLETED: 342/342 synced
```

children.title: list_files()
children.content:
Lists Drive files with optional query filter. Returns the raw Drive API response dict, including `nextPageToken` for caller-driven pagination.

**Parameters:**
- `page_size` (int, default `100`) — number of files per page (Drive max is 1000)
- `page_token` (str, optional) — token from a previous response's `nextPageToken`
- `query` (str, optional) — Drive query string, e.g. `"mimeType='application/pdf'"` or `"name contains 'spec'"`

**Returns:** `Dict` — `{"files": [...], "nextPageToken": "..."}`

```python
page = await connector.list_files(
    page_size=50,
    query="mimeType='application/pdf' and trashed=false",
)
for f in page["files"]:
    print(f["id"], f["name"], f["mimeType"])
next_token = page.get("nextPageToken")
```

children.title: list_folders()
children.content:
Lists only Drive folders by applying a `mimeType = 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder'` filter internally.

**Parameters:**
- `page_size` (int, default `100`)
- `page_token` (str, optional)

**Returns:** `Dict` — `{"files": [...], "nextPageToken": "..."}`

```python
page = await connector.list_folders()
for folder in page["files"]:
    print(folder["id"], folder["name"])
```

children.title: list_shared_drives()
children.content:
Lists all shared drives (formerly Team Drives) accessible to the authenticated user.

**Parameters:**
- `page_size` (int, default `100`)
- `page_token` (str, optional)

**Returns:** `Dict` — `{"drives": [...], "nextPageToken": "..."}`

```python
drives = await connector.list_shared_drives()
for drive in drives["drives"]:
    print(drive["id"], drive["name"])
```

children.title: get_file()
children.content:
Retrieves full metadata for a single Drive file using `fields=*`.

**Parameters:**
- `file_id` (str, required) — the Drive file ID (visible in the file's URL after `/d/`)

**Returns:** `Dict` — complete file resource from the Drive API

```python
meta = await connector.get_file(
    file_id="1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgVE2upms"
)
print(meta["name"], meta["mimeType"], meta["modifiedTime"])
```

children.title: search_files()
children.content:
Searches Drive files using a Drive query string. Returns a flat list (not a paginated dict). Fetches only the first page up to `page_size`.

**Parameters:**
- `query` (str, required) — Drive query string
- `page_size` (int, default `100`)

**Returns:** `List[Dict]`

```python
results = await connector.search_files(
    query="name contains 'Q4 Report' and trashed=false"
)
for f in results:
    print(f["id"], f["name"])
```

children.title: get_permissions()
children.content:
Lists all sharing permissions for a Drive file.

**Parameters:**
- `file_id` (str, required)

**Returns:** `Dict` — Drive permissions list resource

```python
perms = await connector.get_permissions(file_id="1BxiMVs0XRA5...")
for p in perms.get("permissions", []):
    print(p.get("emailAddress"), p["role"])
```

children.title: export_file()
children.content:
Exports a Google Workspace file (Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drawings) to a specified MIME type and returns raw bytes. Only works for native Google file types — binary uploads have their own content and do not need exporting.

**Parameters:**
- `file_id` (str, required) — Drive file ID of a Google Workspace document
- `mime_type` (str, required) — export target, e.g. `"text/plain"`, `"application/pdf"`, `"application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document"`

**Returns:** `bytes`

```python
pdf_bytes = await connector.export_file(
    file_id="1BxiMVs0XRA5...",
    mime_type="application/pdf",
)
with open("document.pdf", "wb") as fh:
    fh.write(pdf_bytes)
```

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Typed exceptions are defined in `exceptions.py`. All public methods wrap HTTP calls with `with_retry()` from `helpers/utils.py`.

### Exception classes

| Exception | HTTP status | Meaning | Connector behaviour |
|-----------|-------------|---------|---------------------|
| `GoogleDriveAuthError` | 401 | Access token invalid or expired | `health_check()` returns `DEGRADED / TOKEN_EXPIRED`; triggers `_do_refresh_token()` or prompts re-authorization |
| `GoogleDriveError` | 4xx / 5xx | API-level error (quota, file not found, server error) | `health_check()` returns `DEGRADED / FAILED`; individual file failures in `sync()` increment `documents_failed` without aborting the sync |

### Retry behaviour
`with_retry()` retries on network-level errors and 5xx responses using exponential back-off. `GoogleDriveAuthError` (401) is **never retried** — it surfaces immediately so the caller can refresh or re-authorize.

### Missing access token
If `health_check()` is called before OAuth is completed, it returns `OFFLINE / MISSING_CREDENTIALS` with message `'No access token — complete OAuth authorization first'`.

### sync() isolation
Individual file normalization failures inside `sync()` increment `documents_failed` but do not abort the sync. The final `SyncStatus` is `PARTIAL` when `documents_failed > 0` and `COMPLETED` when zero failures occurred.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### `install()` returns `MISSING_CREDENTIALS`
**Cause:** `client_id` or `client_secret` is empty or not provided in `config`.
**Fix:** Retrieve both values from Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials → your OAuth 2.0 Client ID and pass them in config.

### `health_check()` returns `TOKEN_EXPIRED` (`GoogleDriveAuthError`)
**Cause:** The stored `access_token` has expired (Google access tokens expire after 1 hour).
**Fix:** Internally `_do_refresh_token()` will obtain a new token using the stored refresh token. If the refresh token itself is missing or revoked, re-run the OAuth flow (`authorize()` → `exchange_code()`).

### `health_check()` returns `OFFLINE` — `No access token`
**Cause:** OAuth was never completed — no `access_token` is in config.
**Fix:** Call `authorize()`, redirect the user to the URL, then call `exchange_code()` with the `code` callback parameter.

### `exchange_code()` fails with `redirect_uri_mismatch`
**Cause:** The `redirect_uri` in config does not exactly match any Authorized Redirect URI registered in Google Cloud Console.
**Fix:** Open Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials → your OAuth 2.0 Client ID → Authorized redirect URIs and add the exact URI used.

### Google Drive API returns `Access Not Configured`
**Cause:** The Google Drive API has not been enabled in the Google Cloud project.
**Fix:** Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Library → search "Google Drive API" → Enable.

### `sync()` has high `documents_failed` count
**Cause:** File normalization is failing for certain file types (e.g. files in Trash, or missing required metadata fields).
**Fix:** Inspect connector logs for exception details. To exclude trashed files, apply the query filter `trashed=false` inside `_collect_all_files()`.

### Shared drive files not appearing in sync
**Cause:** `files.list` excludes shared drives by default.
**Fix:** Use `list_shared_drives()` to enumerate shared drives, then call `list_files()` with `driveId` and `includeItemsFromAllDrives=true` via the `query` argument.

### `export_file()` fails with 403 on a non-Google file
**Cause:** `export_file()` only works for native Google Workspace file types (Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drawings).
**Fix:** Use `get_file()` to retrieve binary files directly; use `export_file()` only for files whose `mimeType` begins with `application/vnd.google-apps.`.
